definition:
1. to bend  or turn aside , παρακλίνας κεφαλήν (Odyssey by Homer); π. τὴν θύραν, τὴν πύλην to set  it ajar , (Herdotus Historicus) 
2. metaphorically, ἄλλῃ παρκλίνουσι δίκας they turn  justice from her path , (Hesiod) 
3. to lay beside  another:—;Pass. to lie down beside , at meals, Lat. accumbere, τινι (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus) 
4. intransitive to turn aside , (Iliad by Homer); παρακλίνασα having swerved from the course , (Aeschulus Tragicus) (ML)
